page.title: SSH Identity & Servers
page.subtitle: Configure your local identity and manage your remote connections securely.
step0.label: STEP 0 · OPTIONAL
step0.title: SSH Server
step0.subtitle: Turn this machine into an SSH host. Other devices on your network can then connect to it. Skip if you only need to connect to remote servers.
enable.title: Enable SSH on This Machine
enable.desc: Starts the SSH daemon and opens port 22 in the firewall (ufw / firewalld). Requires passwordless sudo or polkit access.
enable.waitingAuth: Waiting for system password dialog… Check for a polkit prompt on your desktop. If none appears, start polkit-gnome-authentication-agent-1.
enable.alreadyEnabledDetail: The SSH daemon (sshd) is active on this machine. Remote devices can connect on port 22 if your firewall allows it.
step1.label: STEP 1 · REQUIRED
step1.title: Local Identity Setup
step1.subtitle: Your SSH key is your identity on the network. Generate one here, then copy the public key to any remote server or GitHub/GitLab account you want to access without a password.
generate.title: Generate Local ID
generate.desc: Create a secure SSH key to identify this machine to remote servers.
generate.success: ✅ SSH key ready (no passphrase — stored in ~/.ssh).
identity.title: Identity Options
identity.desc: Copy your public key for manual setup, or use the automatic "Enable Remote Access" button after connecting.
identity.githubPublickeyHelp: Your SSH key exists on this PC, but GitHub does not have it yet. Copy your public key, add it on GitHub, then test again.
identity.githubPublickeySteps: 1. Click Copy Public Key below · 2. On GitHub paste it under Settings → SSH and GPG keys → New SSH key · 3. Click Test GitHub Connection again.
step2.label: STEP 2 · CONNECT
step2.title: Saved Servers
step2.subtitle: Bookmark remote machines by label. Hit Connect to open a live shell, browse files, or push your public key automatically.
bookmark.label: Label
activity.title: Connection History
activity.subtitle: Recent connections and active sessions. Click a session to re-open the terminal or file browser.
activity.empty: No connections yet. Click "Connect" on a saved server.
prereqs.title: 📋 Remote Device Prerequisites
prereqs.desc: The remote machine must have SSH running and port 22 open before you can connect. Run the commands matching its distro:
terminal.hint: Type exit to end the session — window closes automatically
password.title: 🔑 Activate Remote Access
password.desc: Enter the password for {{user}}@{{host}} to enable the file browser.
password.noKey: No SSH key found. Generate one in the Wizard first.
flatpakHint: Flatpak note: if SSH key/files are inaccessible, grant access to `~/.ssh` for the app (for example with `flatpak override --user --filesystem=~/.ssh io.github.karimodora.LinuxDevHome`).
error.authFailed: Authentication failed. Check your keys or password.
error.hostKeyFail: Host key verification failed. The server identity changed or is unknown.
error.timeout: Connection timed out. Ensure the server is reachable.
error.refused: Connection refused. Is SSH running on the remote host?
error.fileNotFound: Local file or SSH identity not found.
error.toolMissing: SSH tools are missing on your host system.
error.noKey: No SSH key found. Please generate one first.
error.hostCommandTimeout: A host command took too long and was stopped.
error.noPolkitAgent: No polkit password dialog is available. Start polkit-gnome-authentication-agent-1 or run the commands in a terminal.
error.githubPublickeyDenied: GitHub does not recognize your key yet — add your public key to your GitHub account first.
wizard.title: Set up SSH step by step
wizard.subtitle: Follow each page in order. You can skip optional steps. Progress is saved when you leave and come back.
wizard.intro.title: What you will set up
wizard.intro.lead: This wizard walks you through SSH identity, GitHub access, and optional server hardening. Each step is one screen — use Next when ready.
wizard.intro.itemKey: Create an SSH key on this PC (your digital identity)
wizard.intro.itemGithub: Register the key on GitHub and verify the connection
wizard.intro.itemServer: Optionally turn this PC into an SSH host for your network
wizard.intro.itemHarden: Optionally disable password SSH login for better security
wizard.key.title: Step 1 — Create your SSH key
wizard.key.lead: Click Generate Key. No passphrase is required — the key is stored in ~/.ssh. If you already have a key, it appears below.
wizard.github.title: Step 2 — Connect to GitHub
wizard.github.lead: GitHub must have your public key before the test can succeed. Permission denied (publickey) means the key is not on GitHub yet — that is normal until you add it.
wizard.github.stepPaste: Open GitHub SSH settings and paste as a new key
wizard.github.stepTest: Click Test GitHub Connection — wait for success before Next
wizard.github.skippedNote: You skipped GitHub setup. You can return to this step from the sidebar anytime.
wizard.server.title: Step 3 — SSH server (optional)
wizard.server.lead: Only needed if other devices should SSH into this PC. Skip if you only push code to GitHub or connect to remote servers.
wizard.server.optional: This step is optional. Use Skip this step if you do not need incoming SSH.
wizard.harden.title: Step 4 — Disable password login (optional)
wizard.harden.lead: After key login works everywhere you need, click the button below to turn off SSH password login on this machine.
wizard.harden.optional: Optional — skip unless you already use key-based SSH and want stricter security.
wizard.harden.success: Password SSH disabled on this machine.
wizard.harden.failed: Could not disable password login. Try the copy command in a terminal.
wizard.harden.copied: Command copied — paste it in a terminal.
wizard.done.title: Setup complete
wizard.done.lead: You finished the wizard. Use SSH manager for bookmarks, terminals, and file transfer.
